questions.question: Express the sum 1 + 4 + 9 + 16 + 25 + ... + 100 using summation notation and evaluate it.
keyPoints: Identify the pattern as perfect squares: i² from i=1 to i=10

questions.question: Find the closed-form expression for the summation Σ(i=1 to n) (2i - 1).
keyPoints: Recognize this as an arithmetic series: 1 + 3 + 5 + ... + (2n-1)

questions.question: Consider the algorithm: for i = 1 to n do for j = 1 to i do x = x + 1. (a) Express the number of operations as a summation. (b) Find the closed-form and determine the time complexity in Big-O notation. (c) If n = 1000, how many times is the inner statement executed?
keyPoints: Inner loop executes i times for each i, total operations = Σ(i=1 to n) i
Time complexity: O(n²) - dominant term is n²/2

questions.question: (a) Prove by induction that Σ(i=1 to n) i = n(n+1)/2. (b) Derive the closed-form formula for Σ(i=0 to n) r^i where r ≠ 1. (c) Apply these results to analyze the time complexity of an algorithm with nested loops where the outer loop runs n times and the inner loop runs 1 + 2 + 4 + 8 + ... + 2^(n-1) times.
keyPoints: Base case n=1: 1 = 1(2)/2 = 1; Inductive step: assume true for k, prove for k+1
Geometric series formula: Σr^i = (r^(n+1)-1)/(r-1) for r≠1
